title: The Snare
content:
(To A.E.)


    I hear a sudden cry of pain!
     There is a rabbit in a snare:
    Now I hear the cry again,
     But I cannot tell from where.

    But I cannot tell from where
     He is calling out for aid;
    Crying on the frightened air,
     Making everything afraid.

    Making everything afraid,
     Wrinkling up his little face,
    As he cries again for aid;
     And I cannot find the place!

    And I cannot find the place
     Where his paw is in the snare:
    Little one! Oh, little one!
     I am searching everywhere.
meaning:
This evocative piece by James Stephens, titled "The Snare", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
